metadata.summaryPolicy: Most summaries are derived from data_prep source table content. Records with summarySource data_prep_and_original_page include only facts visible on the checked original page.

signals.title: UNICEF Burundi Humanitarian Situation Report No. 20, May 2026
signals.cardSummary: In May 2026, Burundi faced population movement and regional health risks. UNICEF reported that 632,989 children needed humanitarian assistance and about 102,000 people remained internally displaced. UNICEF required USD 8.67 million for 2...
signals.detailedSummary: In May 2026, Burundi faced population movement and regional health risks. UNICEF reported that 632,989 children needed humanitarian assistance and about 102,000 people remained internally displaced. UNICEF required USD 8.67 million for 2026, with about USD 5.9 million still unfunded. Education, nutrition, and social protection had large funding gaps. Potential project direction: Emergency child support package: learning materials, hygiene kits, child protection referral, and mobile health screening.Donor proposal package for education recovery, child nutrition, and household protection or savings groups.
signals.keyInformationSummary: In May 2026, Burundi faced population movement and regional health risks. UNICEF reported that 632,989 children needed humanitarian assistance and about 102,000 people remained internally displaced. UNICEF required USD 8.67 million for 2026, with about USD 5.9 million still unfunded. Education, nutrition, and social protection had large funding gaps.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Emergency child support package: learning materials, hygiene kits, child protection referral, and mobile health screening.Donor proposal package for education recovery, child nutrition, and household protection or savings groups.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Burundi Kids because the source concerns children, education, health, nutrition in Burundi, matching its focus on children, education, health, girls and women, and community support.
signals.originalTitle: UNICEF Burundi Humanitarian Situation Report No. 20, May 2026
signals.dateVerificationNote: UNICEF PDF URL and report cover identify Situation Report No. 20 dated 31 May 2026.

signals.title: UNICEF Burundi Humanitarian Situation Report No. 18, March 2026
signals.cardSummary: Mobile clinics in Musenyi II carried out 14,058 malaria rapid tests, with 9,123 positive cases. Newly arrived children under five also received vaccination support. In March 2026, 6,216 children in Busuma received mental health and psych...
signals.detailedSummary: Mobile clinics in Musenyi II carried out 14,058 malaria rapid tests, with 9,123 positive cases. Newly arrived children under five also received vaccination support. In March 2026, 6,216 children in Busuma received mental health and psychosocial support, while additional children received case management. Potential project direction: Refugee child health screening, malaria prevention, vaccination mobilization, and community health volunteer training. Psychosocial support project with child activity spaces, trauma identification, case management, and parent support groups.
signals.keyInformationSummary: Mobile clinics in Musenyi II carried out 14,058 malaria rapid tests, with 9,123 positive cases. Newly arrived children under five also received vaccination support. In March 2026, 6,216 children in Busuma received mental health and psychosocial support, while additional children received case management.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Refugee child health screening, malaria prevention, vaccination mobilization, and community health volunteer training. Psychosocial support project with child activity spaces, trauma identification, case management, and parent support groups.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Burundi Kids because the source concerns children, education, health, public health in Busuma, Musenyi II, Burundi, matching its focus on children, education, health, girls and women, and community support.

signals.title: WeWorld: Nutritional emergency in Busuma refugee camp
signals.cardSummary: In Busuma camp, more than 65,000 people who fled eastern DRC face vulnerable conditions, including nutrition risks for young children and pregnant or breastfeeding women.
signals.detailedSummary: The data_prep table flags a nutritional emergency in Busuma refugee camp. The original WeWorld page confirms that Busuma hosted over 65,000 people who fled eastern DRC, with children under five and pregnant or breastfeeding women especially exposed to malnutrition risks. WeWorld described fortified porridge distribution coordinated with UNHCR, WFP, UNICEF, local authorities, and healthcare partners.
signals.keyInformationSummary: After the DRC refugee influx, Busuma refugee camp faced acute food and nutrition needs. The original WeWorld page reports more than 65,000 people in the camp and identifies children under five and pregnant or breastfeeding women as especially vulnerable.
signals.convertibleProjectDirection: Child nutrition station with porridge distribution, screening for children under five, and caregiver nutrition education.
signals.projectFitReason: Relevant to Burundi Kids because the source concerns child nutrition, refugee families, food security, health monitoring, and humanitarian response in Busuma, Burundi.
signals.originalTitle: WeWorld: Nutritional emergency in Busuma refugee camp
signals.dateVerificationNote: Original page date field found via meta article:published_time: 5 Jun 2026.
